questions.text: The soup _ good
questions.subject: English
questions.explanation: The correct answer is "Tastes." The sentence is incomplete, but to make it grammatically correct, you would say, "The soup tastes good." In this case, "tastes" is the appropriate verb form to describe how the soup is

questions.text: Unless we _ now, we cannot be on time
questions.subject: English
questions.explanation: The correct answer is "start." The sentence is a conditional statement, and "start" is the present verb that fits in the context. It means that unless we start (right now), we cannot be on time.

questions.text: Daud is better than _ of the college.
questions.subject: English
questions.explanation: The complete sentence would be "Daud is better than all other teachers of the college." This means Daud is superior to every other teacher in the college.

questions.text: Abide _ the traffic laws for smooth and safe flow of traffic.
questions.subject: English
questions.explanation: The sentence should be "Abide by the traffic laws for smooth and safe flow of traffic." "By" is the correct preposition to use in this context to indicate compliance with the traffic laws.

questions.text: He prefers death _ dishonor.
questions.subject: English
questions.explanation: To is the correct word to be used here. In this context the man prefers or prioritizes death rather than dishonor.

questions.text: When both the alleles of a gene pair are same, the organism is said to be:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: Homozygous means having two identical alleles for a particular gene, either both dominant or both recessive.

questions.text: What does the word "SURPLUS" mean?
questions.subject: English
questions.explanation: The word surplus is an adjective which means that something is in excess.

questions.text: What does the word "SPILL" mean?
questions.subject: English
questions.explanation: The word spill means spreading over.

questions.subject: English

questions.text: Pick the word with correct spelling:
questions.subject: English

questions.text: Leydig cells are responsible for:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Leydig cells, located in the testes, are responsible for the production and secretion of testosterone, the primary male sex hormone.
Leydig cells do not produce FSH; it is produced by the anterior pituitary gland in the brain and plays a role in regulating sperm production and the maturation of ovarian follicles in females.
Sperm production occurs in the seminiferous tubules of the testes, mainly involving Sertoli cells and germ cells, not Leydig cells.

Leydig cells do not inhibit testosterone; they are responsible for its production. However, testosterone production can be regulated by negative feedback on the hypothalamus and pituitary gland to maintain hormonal balance.

questions.explanation: Leydig cells, located in the testes, are responsible for the production and secretion of testosterone, the primary male sex hormone.

questions.text: Hormone secreted in bulk due to decreased water content of blood is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: ADH is released in response to increased blood osmolality or decreased water content. It helps the body retain water by reducing urine production and increasing water reabsorption in the kidneys.
Oxytocin plays a role in uterine contractions during labor and milk ejection during breastfeeding. It is not directly related to water balance in the blood.
Glucagon is released in response to low blood glucose levels and stimulates the liver to release glucose into the bloodstream. It is not involved in regulating water content.
Thyroxin is a thyroid hormone that regulates metabolism and energy balance. It is not directly related to the regulation of water content in the blood.

questions.explanation: ADH is released in response to increased blood osmolality or decreased water content. It helps the body retain water by reducing urine production and increasing water reabsorption in the kidneys.

questions.text: Na+ (sodium ions) are nearly _ times greater in fluid outside the cell th inside:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: The concentration of Na+ ions is approximately 10 times greater in fluids outside (extracellular) the cell compared to inside (intracellular) the cell. This significant difference in ion concentration plays a crucial role in various physiological processes, such as maintaining the resting membrane potential and facilitating nerve cell function.

questions.text: Pepsinogen is converted into its active form pepsin by:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: Pepsinogen is converted into pepsin by hydrochloric acid.

questions.text: Endorphins are produced in:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Endorphins are neurotransmitters that are produced in various parts of the brain, including the hypothalamus and the pituitary gland. They act as natural painkillers and mood elevators.
The adrenal glands produce hormones such as adrenaline (epinephrine) and cortisol, but they are not the primary site for endorphin production.
The stomach primarily produces digestive enzymes and acids to aid in the digestion of food. It is not the site of endorphin production.
The thymus gland is involved in the development of the immune system, particularly in producing T-lymphocytes (T cells). It is not associated with endorphin production.

questions.explanation: Endorphins are neurotransmitters that are produced in various parts of the brain, including the hypothalamus and the pituitary gland. They act as natural painkillers and mood elevators.

questions.text: Which hormone induces labor pains?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Estrogen helps prepare the body for childbirth by promoting uterine growth and the development of uterine muscle fibers.

Oxytocin is often referred to as the "love hormone" or "cuddle hormone." In the context of childbirth, it plays a crucial role in initiating and regulating uterine smooth muscles contractions during labor. These contractions are essential for the progression of labor and eventually lead to the delivery of the baby.
Progesterone supports a pregnancy by maintaining the uterine lining and preventing premature contractions. Its levels decrease as labor approaches.
LH is not directly involved in labor; it's associated with the menstrual cycle and ovulation, triggering the release of an egg from the ovaries.

questions.explanation: Oxytocin is often referred to as the "love hormone" or "cuddle hormone." In the context of childbirth, it plays a crucial role in initiating and regulating uterine contractions during labor. These contractions are essential for the progression of labor and eventually lead to the delivery of the baby.

questions.text: Site for the production of neurotransmitter is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The postsynaptic neuron receives neurotransmitters released by the presynaptic neuron and contains receptors for these neurotransmitters on it's surface in contact with the synaptic cleft.

Neurotransmitters are synthesized and stored within the presynaptic neuron, specifically in structures called synaptic vesicles. When an action potential reaches the presynaptic neuron, these vesicles release neurotransmitters into the synapse.
Synapses are the junctions where communication occurs between neurons. They consist of both presynaptic and postsynaptic elements.

Dendrites are the branching extensions of neurons that receive signals from other neurons. While they play a critical role in transmitting signals within the neuron, they are not the primary site of neurotransmitter production.

questions.explanation: Neurotransmitters are synthesized and stored within the presynaptic neuron, specifically in structures called synaptic vesicles. When an action potential reaches the presynaptic neuron, these vesicles release neurotransmitters into the synapse.

questions.text: The estrogen hormone secretion during oogenesis is stimulated by:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: LH plays a role in triggering ovulation and the formation of the corpus luteum, but it does not directly stimulate estrogen secretion during oogenesis.

Inhibin is produced by the granulosa cells of developing ovarian follicles and acts as a negative feedback signal to inhibit FSH secretion. While it indirectly influences estrogen levels by regulating FSH, it is not a direct stimulator of estrogen production.
FSH is responsible for stimulating the growth and development of ovarian follicles in the ovaries. These developing follicles, as they mature, produce estrogen.
Testosterone is a male sex hormone, and while small amounts are present in females, it is not a primary stimulator of estrogen secretion during oogenesis.

questions.explanation: FSH is responsible for stimulating the growth and development of ovarian follicles in the ovaries. These developing follicles, as they mature, produce estrogen.

questions.text: Digestion of the food component start from oral cavity?
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: Digestion of carbohydrates begins in the oral cavity with the action of salivary amylase.

questions.text: The organ which are similar in function but differ in structure are called?
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: Analogous organs are similar in function but different in structure. These organs have evolved independently in different species to perform similar functions in response to similar environmental pressures, even though their internal structures are distinct.

questions.text: The hormone responsible for production of sperm cells and male secondary sexual characteristics is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Estrogen is the primary female sex hormone, but small amounts are also present in males. It is not responsible for male secondary characteristics or sperm cell production.
Progesterone is another female sex hormone, and its primary role is in regulating the menstrual cycle and supporting pregnancy. It is not responsible for male characteristics or sperm production.

Testosterone is the primary male sex hormone responsible for the development of male secondary sexual characteristics, such as facial hair, deepening of the voice, and muscle growth. It also plays a crucial role in spermatogenesis (sperm cell production).
Thyroxin is a thyroid hormone that regulates metabolism and energy balance in the body. It is not directly related to male sexual characteristics or sperm cell production.

questions.explanation: Testosterone is the primary male sex hormone responsible for the development of male secondary sexual characteristics, such as facial hair, deepening of the voice, and muscle growth. It also plays a crucial role in spermatogenesis (sperm cell production).

questions.text: Which of the following hormones of the pituitary gland regulate the menstrual cycle?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Estrogen is produced by the developing ovarian follicles and plays a role in the menstrual cycle, but it is LH, not estrogen, that directly triggers ovulation.
While LH and estrogen are both involved in the menstrual cycle, it's the combination of FSH and LH that more precisely regulates the cycle.

FSH and LH, both produced by the anterior pituitary gland, regulate the menstrual cycle. FSH stimulates the growth of ovarian follicles, and LH triggers ovulation and the formation of the corpus luteum. Together, they coordinate the development and release of eggs from the ovaries.
Estrogen and progesterone are important hormonal players in the menstrual cycle, but they are not hormones produced by the pituitary gland; instead, they are primarily produced by the ovaries.

questions.explanation: FSH and LH, both produced by the anterior pituitary gland, regulate the menstrual cycle. FSH stimulates the growth of ovarian follicles, and LH triggers ovulation and the formation of the corpus luteum. Together, they coordinate the development and release of eggs from the ovaries.

questions.text: Which may NOT be a mode of action of an antibody?
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: Antibodies primarily work by neutralizing antigens, precipitating antigens, and enhancing phagocytosis. While antibodies play a role in immune signaling, the direct secretion of cytokines is not a typical mode of action of antibodies.

questions.text: This theory said "mitochondria and chloroplasts are, In effect, ancient bacteria which now live inside the larger cells"?
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: This statement aligns with the endosymbiotic theory, which suggests that mitochondria and chloroplasts were once free-living bacteria that were engulfed by ancestral eukaryotic cells. This theory is not directly associated with Lamarckism or Darwin's theory of evolution.

questions.text: Which of the following traits is transmitted directly from an affected father to only his son?
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: Y-linked traits are passed from father to son through the Y chromosome. They are not present on the X chromosome and are specific to male inheritance

questions.text: Intrinsic factor is secreted by:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: Intrinsic factor is secreted by the stomach and is essential for the absorption of vitamin B12 in the small intestine.

questions.text: The cells which play very important role in developing immunity are:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: Lymphocytes are white blood cells that are also one of th·e body's main types of immune cells. They are made in the bone marrow and found in the blood and lymph tissue. The immune system is a complex network of cells known as immune cells that include lymphocytes.

questions.text: Under activity of parathyroid glands causes a drop in blood of:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: Parathyroid glands play a crucial role in regulating calcium levels in the blood. Underactivity of these glands can lead to a decrease in blood calcium levels

questions.text: Vaccination is an example of:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: Vaccination involves introducing antibodies (acquired) or antibodies-producing agents (artificial) into the body to provide active immunity.

questions.text: The heart chamber from where aorta originates:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: The left ventricle is the heart chamber that pumps oxygenated blood into the aorta, which then carries it to the rest of the body.

questions.text: Cartilage is more difficult to heal than bone because cartilage:
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Has less number of cells deep down

questions.explanation: Cartilage is more difficult to heal than bone primarily because it lacks a vascular supply, meaning it has very limited blood flow. Vascular supply is crucial for the transportation of nutrients, immune cells, and growth factors needed for the healing process. Without a good blood supply, cartilage has a limited ability to repair itself compared to bone, which has a well-developed blood supply to support its healing and regeneration. The other options mentioned also play a role in cartilage's limited healing capacity. For instance, option B is partially true, as cartilage has fewer cells in its deeper layers, which can hinder the healing process. However, the lack of a vascular supply is the most significant factor contributing to the difficulty in cartilage healing.

questions.text: Which feature is absent from cardiac muscles?
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: Cardiac muscle: It is a contractile tissue present in the heart. The muscle fibres are cylindrical, branched and uninucleate. The branches join to form a network called as intercalated disc which are unique distinguishing features of the cardiac muscles.

questions.text: Synthesis of ATP during photosynthesis takes place at the region:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: This is where the components of the electron transport chain are located.

questions.text: Which hormone is chemically a steroid?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: ADH or Vasopressin is essentially a polypeptide hormone.
Thyroxine is essentially an amino acid derivative.
Cortisone is essentially a steroid hormone.
Insulin is essentially a protein hormone.

questions.explanation: Steroid hormones are fat-soluble molecules made from cholesterol. Among these are the three major sex hormone groups: estrogens, androgens, and progesterones. Males and females make all three, just in different amounts. The steroids that are made almost exclusively in the adrenal glands are cortisol, 11-deoxycortisol, aldosterone, corticosterone, and 11-deoxycorticosterone. Most other steroid hormones, including estrogens, are made by the adrenal glands and the gonads.

questions.text: Pubic symphysis and inter vertebral disc are the exmaple of:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: Both pubic symphysis and intervertebral discs are types of cartilaginous joints.

questions.text: Most enzymes work the best at the following temperature:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: Enzymes generally have an optimal temperature at which they exhibit their highest activity. At this temperature (40°C in this case), enzymes work most efficiently because the rate of their catalytic reactions is at its peak. This is because higher temperatures usually provide the necessary kinetic energy for enzyme-substrate interactions to occur with greater frequency and effectiveness.

questions.text: Sarcoplasmic reticulum of muscle fibers is mainly responsible for:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: The sarcoplasmic reticulum of muscle fibers is mainly responsible for "Calcium storage." It plays a crucial role in regulating calcium ion levels within muscle cells, which is essential for muscle contraction and relaxation. When a muscle is stimulated, calcium ions are released from the sarcoplasmic reticulum, allowing the muscle to contract.

questions.text: The junction between two neurons is:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: A synapse is a specialized junction between two neurons (nerve cells) where they communicate. Neurotransmitters are released from one neuron and received by receptors on another neuron at the synapse, allowing for the transmission of nerve impulses.

questions.text: The complete, mature, and infectious virus particle is known as:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: The complete, mature, and infectious virus particle is known as a virion

questions.text: Which of the following proteins establishes the matrix of bone & cartilage?
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: Collagen is a fibrous protein that provides structural support and forms the matrix of tissues like bone and cartilage.

questions.text: Non-competitive inhibitors react with enzymes at:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: Non-competitive inhibitors bind to enzymes at allosteric sites, which are distinct from the active site. This binding alters the enzyme's shape and reduces its activity.

questions.text: In term of enzyme actions, 'maximum temperature' refers to a temperature at which:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: The term "maximum temperature" means the point at which enzymes start to denature (Option A). Denaturation is when enzymes lose their shape and stop working due to excessive heat.

questions.text: Which of the following molecules are released after completion of light reaction and then utilized in the dark reaction of photosynthesis?
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: After the completion of light reactions in photosynthesis, the molecules released and utilized in the dark reactions are ATP and NADPH (Option D). These molecules provide the energy and reducing power required for the Calvin cycle, which is part of the dark reactions.

questions.text: _ reduce the enzymes productivity by blocking the sun entering into the active site due to similar shapes:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: Enzymes' productivity is reduced by blocking the active site due to similar shapes, is associated with "competitive inhibitors." Competitive inhibitors are molecules that closely resemble the substrate and compete with it for binding to the enzyme's active site. When they bind to the active site, they prevent the substrate from binding and, as a result, reduce enzyme activity

questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Capsules protect bacteria from the immune system and promote their attachment to host tissues.
The bacterial cell wall performs several functions as well, in addition to providing overall strength to the cell. It also helps maintain the cell shape,
It protects the bacterial cell from physical damage such as desiccation and antibiotics.
The cell membrane acts as a semipermbeable membrane allowing selective materials to pass across it.

questions.text: When light falls on P-700, which event is likely to occur?
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: When light falls on P-700, it is oxidised. The electrons are removed.

questions.text: Which are the end products of light reactions of photosynthesis?
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: These molecules are generated during the light reactions and are used in the subsequent dark reactions to convert carbon dioxide into glucose.

questions.explanation: All viruses are obligate intracellular parasites.

questions.explanation: What distinguishes Prokaryotic cell walls from Fungal cell walls is that Prokaryotic cell walls contain peptidoglycan

options.text: In between two primary cell walls
Between the primary cell wall and plasma membrane

questions.explanation: Secondary cell walls in plants are present between the primary cell wall and the plasma membrane

questions.text: Select the one which is NOT a function of Smooth Endoplasmic Reticulum (SER)?
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: SER is primarily involved in lipid metabolism, transport of materials, and transmission of impulses.

questions.text: Thermal stability of organisms in the environment is because of which characteristic of water?
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: Water can absorb and retain heat, which helps stabilize the temperature in living organisms.

questions.text: Cell membrane also contains _ place by active and passive transport:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: Charged pores, also known as ion channels, are essential components of the cell membrane that facilitate the exchange of materials and play a crucial role in active and passive transport. Option B, "Corner Proteins," is not a recognized component of the cell membrane, and it should have been "Carrier Proteins" instead. Lipids (option A) form the basic structural framework of the membrane, and processing of glycoproteins (option D) is related to post-translational modification but not primarily responsible for material exchange or transport. Thank you for pointing out the correction.

questions.text: Most abundant organic compound in mammalian cells are:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: The most abundant organic compound in mammalian cells is protein

questions.text: _ are spherical sacs, surrounded by a single membrane and contain hydrolytic enzymes:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: The organelles that are spherical sacs, surrounded by a single membrane, and contain hydrolytic enzymes are "Lysosomes." Lysosomes are involved in the digestion and recycling of cellular waste materials and damaged organelles. They contain various hydrolytic enzymes that break down substances within the cell.

questions.text: C-H bonds in lipids are important:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: Lipids are an energy-rich macromolecule, and the carbon-hydrogen bonds in lipids store a lot of energy.

questions.text: Thermal conductivity of metals is due to:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The layered structure of metals has no effect on thermal conductivity as the conduction through metals is carried out with the assistance of free-moving electrons.

The thermal conductivity of any physical material is a measure of its potential to conduct heat. The freely moving electrons are responsible for the conduction of heat and electricity in the metal. The delocalized electrons in the transfer of the metal kinetic energy; this is also referred to as conduction.
No matter how loosely the metal atoms are held, they cannot move about freely to conduct heat or electricity. If so they won’t be metals (solids), but rather liquids.
The vibrational movement of metals can only transfer the energy to the nearby metal atom, not along the whole metal. Thus, this option is also incorrect.

questions.explanation: The thermal conductivity of any physical material is a measure of its potential to conduct heat. The freely moving electrons are responsible for the conduction of heat and electricity in the metal. The delocalized electrons in the transfer of the metal kinetic energy; this is also referred to as conduction.

questions.text: The amount of energy associated with quantum of radiation is directly proportional to:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Photons are packets of energy that are also called quanta. The amount of energy associated with quantum of radiation is not related to the photon. Hence, this option is incorrect.

Wavelength is the distance between identical points (adjacent crests) in the adjacent cycles of a waveform signal propagated in space. Any change in the wavelength has no effect on the energy emitted by the body.
By the equation; E=hf, we can see that the energy is directly proportional to that of frequency. Hence, this is the correct option.
Velocity/ speed have no relationship with that of the energy of radiation. Hence, this option is also incorrect.

questions.explanation: According to E = hf, it can be seen that 'E' (energy of quantum) is directly proportional to 'f' (frequency). Velocity, photon, and wavelength have no effect on the energy of quantum.

questions.text: Total number of directions of f-orbitals in space are:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: F orbital has 7 directions in space

questions.text: The electronic configuration of degenerate orbitals is explained by:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Hund’s rule explains the electronic configuration of degenerate orbitals

questions.text: The SI unit for pressure is :
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Pascal is the SI unit of Pressure.

questions.text: If value of azimuthal quantum number is 2 then total values of magnetic qunatum number will be:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Option B shows the correct value of magnetic quantum number when azimuthal quantum number is 2, the m value is: -2, -1 , 0 , +1 , +2

questions.text: The idea that molecules in gases are in constant movement is called:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: KMT of gases suggests that idea that molecules are in constant motion

questions.text: Which of the following quantum number is not obtained from Schrodinger Wave equation?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Spin quantum number is not explained through schrodinger’s wave equation, whereas all other quantum number are explained according to that.

questions.text: If both temperature and volume of gas are doubled, the pressure:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Temp is directly proportionsl to pressure and pressure is inversely proportional to volume. So if temp increases pressure will be double and volume decreases pressure comes back to normal state.

questions.text: If 42J heat is transferred to the system during expansion, what is the change in internal energy when work done is 32J?
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: The change in internal energy (ΔU) can be calculated using the first law of thermodynamics: ΔU = Q - W Where: * ΔU is the change in internal energy. * Q is the heat added to the system (42 J in this case). * W is the work done by the system (32 J in this case). So, ΔU = 42 J - 32 J = 10 J. The change in internal energy is 10 Joules. This means that the internal energy of the system increased by 10 J due to the heat added and the work done.

questions.text: In parallel combination of two capacitors, their equivalent capacitance is equal to:
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: When capacitors are connected in parallel, their equivalent capacitance is simply the sum of the individual capacitances.

questions.text: The S.I unit capacitance of a capacitors is:
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: Capacitance is measured in Farads (F) in the International System of Units (SI).

questions.text: In isochoric process:
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: In an isochoric process, also known as an isovolumetric process, the volume remains constant. This means that the system undergoing the process doesn't experience any change in its volume throughout the entire process. In other words, the system is confined within a container or a closed space, and there is no work done in terms of expansion or compression.

questions.text: While studying charging and discharging of a capacitor, Rc = Resistance x Capacitance is known as?
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: In the context of charging and discharging a capacitor, the term "Re" stands for the time constant of the circuit, which is calculated by multiplying the resistance (R) and the capacitance (C). This time constant represents how quickly the capacitor charges or discharges, and it's crucial in understanding the time behavior of the circuit.

questions.text: Volt x Ampere is the unit of:
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: Volt multiplied by Ampere equals Watt, which is the unit of power.

questions.text: The 1st law of thermodynamics is the generalization of the law of conservation of:
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: The first law of thermodynamics is an extension of the conservation of energy, stating that energy cannot be created or destroyed in a closed system, only converted between different forms like heat and work. It's a fundamental principle in thermodynamics.

questions.text: when charged particle enters the magnetic field parallel, it will:
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: In this orientation, the magnetic force on the particle is perpendicular to its velocity, causing it to move in a straight line without any deflection.

questions.text: 1 kWh =
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: 1 kilowatt-hour (kWh) is equivalent to 3.6 million joules (36 * 105 J). It's a unit of energy often used for electricity consumption.

questions.text: The lenz's law is also a statement of the law of conservation of:
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: Lenz's law is also a statement of the law of conservation of energy. It articulates that the direction of the induced electromotive force (emf) or current in a circuit opposes the change in magnetic flux, which is a practical application of the principle of energy conservation.

questions.text: If length of the wire becomes two times to its orignal value and area becomes one half to its orignal value, than resistance of the wire becomes:
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: Resistance (R) is directly proportional to the length (L) and inversely proportional to the cross-sectional area (A) of the wire. If length doubles (2x) and area reduces to half (1/2x), the resistance becomes (2x) / (1/2x) = 4 times the original resistance

questions.text: The dimension of magnetic field strength is same as that of:
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: The dimensions of magnetic field strength (B) are the same as those of magnetic induction, which is Newtons per Ampere-meter (N/A·m).

questions.text: In fleming's right hand rule, the second finger indicates:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: In Fleming's Right-Hand Rule, the second finger indicates the direction of the Current (I).To recall Fleming's Right-Hand Rule:1. Thumb (Th): Direction of Motion (or Force)2. Second finger (Se): Direction of Current (I)3. Forefinger (Fo): Direction of Magnetic Field (B)By pointing your thumb, second finger, and forefinger at right angles to each other, you can determine the direction of each component in a magnetic field.

questions.explanation: Lenz's law states that the direction of the induced current in a circuit is such that it opposes the change in magnetic flux, which is a practical application of the conservation of energy.

questions.text: The weber is unit of measure of:
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: The explanation of this question will be added soon!

questions.text: A device that converts AC into DC is called:
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: A diode is commonly used to convert alternating current (AC) into direct current (DC) in electronic circuits

questions.text: A current generator is a device that converts:
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: A current generator, generates current by converting mechanical energy into electrical energy.

questions.text: The conversion of alternating current into direct current is called:
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: Conversion of alternating current into direct current is called "Rectification" (Option B)

questions.text: In full-wave rectification, _ diodes are used.
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: In full-wave rectification, typically, 4 diodes are used. It allows for the conversion of both the positive and negative halves of the AC waveform into DC, resulting in a smoother DC output.

questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Calculation suggests this option is incorrect

According to Newton's second law in terms of momentum, F=Δp/t where F is the applied force Δp is the change in momentum t is the time taken so Fxt=Δp, the rate of change of momentum. The formula for velocity is displacement/time.

questions.explanation: Change in Momentum= Force x time

questions.text: The time taken for half the number of atoms of radioactive isotopes to missing is called:
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: Radioactive materials consist of unstable atoms that decay over time, emitting radiation in the process. The rate of decay for each radioactive isotope is unique and can be characterized by its half-life.

questions.text: If the car is slowing down along negative x axis than acceleration will be along:
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: The negative acceleration (deceleration) opposes the car's motion along the positive x-axis.

questions.text: At what point during the motion of projectile its vertical component of velocity is zero?
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: The vertical component of velocity becomes zero at the highest point in the projectile's motion, just before it starts descending

questions.text: The instantaneous velocity along the curved path is:
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: The tangent to the curve at a given point represents the direction of instantaneous velocity at that point.

questions.text: A 32g radioactive elements decays and remains 2g after 60 days. What is half-life of this radioactive elements?
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: The half-life of a radioactive element is the time it takes for half of the initial quantity to decay. In this case, the element started with 32g and remains 2g after 60 days. To find the half-life, we can calculate how many times the initial quantity must halve to reach 2g: 32g → 16g (halved once) → 8g (halved twice) → 4g (halved three times) → 2g (halved four times) So, the element halved four times to reach 2g. Since this took 60 days, the half-life is: 60 days / 4 = 15 days

questions.text: Range of a projectile on a horizontal plane is same fo the following pair of angle:
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: For a given initial velocity, the range is the same for angles that add up to 90 degrees, such as 75° and 15°.

questions.text: The slope of displacement-time graph is equal to:
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: The slope represents the rate of change of displacement with respect to time, which is the definition of velocity.

questions.text: In a perfectly elastic collision:
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Momentum, kinetic energy, and total energy are all conserved.

questions.explanation: In such collisions, not only is momentum conserved, but the kinetic energy and total energy of the system also remain constant before and after the collision, making it a special case of collision behavior.

questions.text: The area under a force-displacement graph gives:
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: The area under a force-displacement graph represents the work done by the force on an object.

questions.text: Power is dot product of:
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: Power (P) is calculated as the dot product of force (F) and velocity (v), which represents the rate at which work is done.

questions.text: In case of centripetal force the value of instantaneous acceleration is given by:
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: The instantaneous acceleration (a) in the case of centripetal force can be calculated using the formula a = v2 / r, where: "a" is the acceleration. "v" is the velocity of the object. "r" is the radius of the circular path.

questions.text: The object has 1 J of P.E. What is the work done in terms of height?
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: The work done in terms of height to give an object 1 Joule (J) of potential energy is 1 J. So, the correct answer is: 1 J

questions.text: A body of mass 'm' is moving with velocity 'v'. After a short interval of time its velocity becomes double. How many times its K.E will increase or decrease?
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: Kinetic energy is proportional to the square of velocity, so if velocity doubles, K.E increases 4 times.

questions.text: The maximum displacement of particles of a medium, on either side of the mean position of a wave, is called:
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: The amplitude of a wave is the maximum displacement of particles on either side of the equilibrium or mean position of the wave. It represents the wave's intensity or strength.

questions.text: An elecric motor turns at 400 revolutions per minute. Its angular velocity in rad/s will be:
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: Explanation for this question will be added soon.

questions.text: The relation between radian and degree is:
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: 1 radian is approximately equal to 57.3 degrees. This is the conversion factor between radians and degrees.

questions.text: the formula of centripetal acceleration is
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: The formula for centripetal acceleration is Fc=mrω², hence, Option C is correct.

questions.text: With increase in pressure, the speed of sound will:
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: In general, an increase or decrease in pressure has no effect on the speed of sound.

questions.text: A disc, a hoop and a sphere are rolling down from an inclined plane simultaneously. Which object will reach at the bottom first?
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: If a hoop, a sphere and a disc are rolling down from an inclined plane simultaneously, the sphere will reach the bottom first.

questions.text: In the following figure the wavelength is:
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: Two waveforms are shown, the total wavelengths being 2 which leads to the individual wavelength being 1 m

questions.text: In transverse waves, the portion above the mean level is called:
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: The wave crest is the highest point of a transverse wave, above the mean level.
